chief_complaint: A 76-year-old man presented with refractory generalized seizures that progressed to nonconvulsive status epilepticus after elective revision cranioplasty.
history: The patient underwent revision titanium cranioplasty due to symptomatic subsidence of a previously implanted acrylic plate. He had no prior history of seizures, and his medical history was unremarkable. He was not taking any antiepileptic medications.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed generalized tonic-clonic seizures.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: CT scan of the head showed a titanium cranioplasty plate in place. Other tests: EEG showed generalized seizure activity.
correct_diagnosis: Refractory Status Epilepticus after Elective Revision Cranioplasty
differentials: Refractory Status Epilepticus after Elective Revision Cranioplasty
correct_ranking: Refractory Status Epilepticus after Elective Revision Cranioplasty
